verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family member that is constitutively cytosolic in healthy cells and translocates to mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM) upon apoptotic stress. It has no TM anchor, GPI anchor, or documented stable surface-partner mechanism. The protein functions exclusively as an intracellular apoptotic effector via mitochondrial MOMP. No evidence supports BAX reaching the extracellular-facing leaflet of the plasma membrane under any documented condition (baseline, stress, cancer, cell death, etc.).
